title: Practising sounds and letters in the first 6 months - Ministry of Education
content:
## Practising sounds and letters in the first 6 months
Use these slides to help you practise sounds and letters (phonics) with your child.

We’re inviting parents and caregivers to share their feedback on the Parent Portal to help us improve the information and support we provide. Take a few minutes to complete our short survey.
Parent Portal survey – Survey Monkey

## Practising sounds and letters with your child #
These slides have the sounds and letters that are taught in the first 6 months of school. You can use them at home to practise the sounds and letters your child has been learning at school.
The slides are best viewed full screen. Select the 3 dots at the bottom of the slides and then select 'Enter full screen'. These resources may not be accessible.
🔊 Select this icon to hear the name of the letter, the sound being learned and a word containing that sound. It can take a few seconds to play.
/ / These slashes indicate the sound for the letter or letters. For example, / m / is represented by the letter m .
- This hyphen indicates that the letters occur at the end of a word.

## Sounds and letters cards – Set 1 #
Set 1 includes the letters m , a , d , p , o , t , n , i , s , and the sounds they represent.
- Your child has been learning the /m/ sound. The letter for this is m.
- Listen for the /m/ sound at the beginning of these words: mouse mug mountain. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the /m/ sound at the end of these words: ham room swim. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /m/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / a/ short vowel sound. The letter for this is a.
- Listen for the / a/ sound at the beginning of these words: apple ant alligator. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /a/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / d/ sound. The letter for this is d.
- Listen for the / d/ sound at the beginning of these words: dog dinosaur dance. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the / d/ sound at the end of these words: red pad bird. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /d/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / p/ sound. The letter for this is p.
- Listen for the / p/ sound at the beginning of these words: pot past puzzle. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the / p/ sound at the end of these words: tip nap cup. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /p/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / o/ short vowel sound. The letter for this is o.
- Listen for the / o/ sound at the beginning of these words: octopus odd orange. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /o/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / t/ sound. The letter for this is t.
- Listen for the / t/ sound at the beginning of these words: tent tiger top. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the / t/ sound at the end of these words: hat boat fruit. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /t/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the /n/ sound. The letter for this is n.
- Listen for the /n/ sound at the beginning of these words: nest nice number. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the /n/ sound at the end of these words: train sun green. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /n/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the /i/ short vowel sound. The letter for this is i.
- Listen for the /i/ sound at the beginning of these words: insect igloo itch. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /i/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the /s/ sound. The letter for this is s.
- Listen for the /s/ sound at the beginning of these words: sun snowman sit. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the /s/ sound at the end of these words: bus class mess. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /s/ sound.

## Sounds and letters cards – Set 2 #
Set 2 includes the letters l , e , c , f , u , b , g , r , h , and the sounds they represent.
- Your child has been learning the / l/ sound. The letter for this is l.
- Listen for the / l/ sound at the beginning of these words: lion light ladder. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the / l/ sound at the end of these words: ball girl hill. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /l/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / e/ short vowel sound. The letter for this is e.
- Listen for the / e/ sound at the beginning of these words: egg edge ever. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /e/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / k/ sound. The letter for this is c.
- Listen for the / k/ sound at the beginning of these words: cat cold cooking. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /k/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / f/ sound. The letter for this is f.
- Listen for the / f/ sound at the beginning of these words: fish feet finger. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the / f/ sound at the end of these words: half leaf roof. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /f/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / u/ short vowel sound. The letter for this is u.
- Listen for the / u/ sound at the beginning of these words: umbrella up under. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /u/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / b/ sound. The letter for this is b.
- Listen for the / b/ sound at the beginning of these words: bird banana bear. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the / b/ sound at the end of these words: grab web club. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /b/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / g/ sound. The letter for this is g.
- Listen for the / g/ sound at the beginning of these words: goat gum gorilla. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the / g/ sound at the end of these words: bag egg dog. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /g/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / r/ sound. The letter for this is r.
- Listen for the / r/ sound at the beginning of these words: rainbow rock ride. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /r/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the /h/ sound. The letter for this is h.
- Listen for the /h/ sound at the beginning of these words: house hand heater. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /h/ sound.

## Sounds and letters cards – Set 3 #
Set 3 includes letters k , j , v , w , y , z , and groups of letters -ck, th, ch, sh, -ng and the sounds they represent.
- Your child has been learning the /k/ sound. The letter for this is k.
- Listen for the /k/ sound at the beginning of these words: key kite kiwi. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /k/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the /j/ sound. The letter for this is j.
- Listen for the /j/ sound at the beginning of these words: jelly juice jacket. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /j/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the /v/ sound. The letter for this is v.
- Listen for the /v/ sound at the beginning of these words: van voice volcano. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the /v/ sound at the end of these words: give have glove. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /v/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the /w/ sound. The letter for this is w.
- Listen for the /w/ sound at the beginning of these words: watch watermelon world. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /w/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the /y/ sound. The letter for this is y.
- Listen for the /y/ sound at the beginning of these words: yoyo yellow yes. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /y/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the / z/ sound. The letter for this is z.
- Listen for the / z/ sound at the beginning of these words: zip zebra zoo. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the / z/ sound at the end of these words: fizz buzz quiz. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /z/ sound.
- Your child has been learning the ck spelling pattern for the /k/ sound at the end of short words.
- Listen for the /k/ sound at the end of these words: lock quack check. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that end with the /k/ sound.
- Your child has been learning that the 2 letters th represent the sound /th/.
- Listen for the /th/ sound at the beginning of these words: thumb thick that. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the /th/ sound at the end of these words: moth bath earth. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /th/ sound.
- Your child has been learning that the 2 letters ch represent the sound /ch/.
- Listen for the /ch/ sound at the beginning of these words: chick chain cheese. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the /ch/ sound at the end of these words: lunch torch couch. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /ch/ sound.
- Your child has been learning that the 2 letters sh represent the sound /sh/.
- Listen for the /sh/ sound at the beginning of these words: sheep shadow shoe. Say them with your child.
- Listen for the /sh/ sound at the end of these words: fish brush dash. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that have the /sh/ sound.
- Your child has been learning that the 2 letters ng represent the sound /ng/.
- Listen for the /ng/ sound at the end of these words: wing song lung. Say them with your child.
- Help your child to find a few things in the house that end with the /ng/ sound.
Practice is important when learning sounds and letters.
Practising the sounds your child has learned, for up to 10 minutes a day, will help them to remember what has been taught at school.
When your child is learning to read, their teacher will show them how sounds match with letters and how to combine these to read words. They will start with simple sounds and letters, then move on to more complex ones.
The sets of slides move from simple to more complex following the Ready to Read Phonics Plus series (provided by the Ministry of Education). Practise the set that best matches what your child has learned at school.
Play games with your child that focus on the sounds and letters in words.
- Go on a hunt to find things starting with the / s / sound.
- Say, "I am thinking of an animal that starts with the / t / sound" ( tiger ).
- Show your child everyday objects and identify what the first sound is. You can match the sound to the letter, for example, bag / b / b .
- Show your child how sounds are linked to letters by pointing out words in your home and in your community, for example, words in books or on signs.
- Encourage them to practise writing their name.
- Show them that they can use their sound-to-letter knowledge to spell words.
